aboutPage.heroBody: The designer who tells you to slow down.
aboutPage.whoIAm:
I'm Cherry — a Vietnamese designer studying Design and Visual Communications at SCAD, and I make things that ask people to slow down.

My recurring subject is **time and memory**: waiting for coffee to brew, revisiting an old reflection, reclaiming the minutes we spend standing in line. A lot of that comes from home — my grandpa waiting for his coffee on the porch in Vietnam, Sài Gòn coffee talk, the unhurried pace of Đà Nẵng. Where I come from shows up in what I make.

Before SCAD I was a yearbook design lead and did theatre — writing, stage design, acting — so I think like a storyteller first and build product second. I also draw characters (say hi to Wally), which keeps an illustrator's warmth in my work.

*Design with empathy* isn't a tagline I decorate with. It's the whole argument.

experience.desc: Hosted by Innovative Design @ USC, theme "Design Against Efficiency." Built Everloop with Aarya Mahapatra — memory architecture for keeping New Year's resolutions.
Designed UI components and shaped the visual identity for an app that turns in-between moments into meaningful actions.

Where most of the work on this site was born.

caseStudies.title: Aurora
caseStudies.oneliner: A jewelry brand where fashion, legacy, and technology meet.
caseStudies.pullQuote: A locket can be a music box of memory.

sections.headline: Jewelry that carries more than light
sections.body: Aurora is a jewelry brand built on the belief that fashion, legacy, and technology can meet in one object. The centerpiece is a locket — a *music box of memory* — a piece you wear because of what it holds, not just how it catches light.
I built the brand system end to end: brand message, tone of voice, logo and logo color, typography, 3D product renders, and the app interface that lives alongside the physical pieces.

sections.headline: Inspiring. Comforting. Futuristic.
sections.body: The voice balances three registers — inspiring, comforting, futuristic — held together by consistency, authenticity, and connection. Every slide, label, and screen had to sound like the same warm, forward-looking brand.

The identity pairs an elegant italic serif with lush coral-to-peach gradients and halftone floral texture, carried across the deck, the 3D renders of the locket and necklace, and the app screens.

*TODO(cherry): add deck frames, renders, and app screens.*

caseStudies.title: Everloop
caseStudies.oneliner: Turning past experiences into future motivation — Record, Reflect, Revisit.
caseStudies.award: 1st place — CreateSC 2026 UI/UX Designathon, hosted by Innovative Design @ USC

sections.headline: Resolutions fade because we design for efficiency
sections.body: The designathon theme was *Design Against Efficiency*. We looked at New Year's resolutions — how they start with real feeling and dissolve into guilt — and asked what would happen if, instead of optimizing people toward their goals, we helped their own past experiences become the motivation.

sections.headline: Memory architecture
sections.body: We designed for how content *gains meaning over time*. A note you write today is a seed; the product's job is to bring it back to you at the moment it matters, grown into encouragement.

sections.headline: Record → Reflect → Revisit
sections.body: Everloop is built on the three R's. **Record** captures moments as scrapbook-style cards. **Reflect** turns them over gently — a daily tracker, prompts that ask rather than demand. **Revisit** closes the loop: *words from yourself*, delivered back like an envelope from someone who believes in you.

Everloop took **1st place** at the CreateSC 2026 UI/UX Designathon, hosted by Innovative Design @ USC.

*TODO(cherry): add app screens — Record / Reflect / Revisit cards, daily tracker, the envelope.*

caseStudies.title: Mugmood
caseStudies.oneliner: A journaling app that turns time waiting for coffee into a moment for reflection.
caseStudies.pullQuote: Whenever everything feels fast, choose slow.

sections.headline: What do we do with the waiting?
sections.body: Mugmood began with a SCAD ClubHouse "Async" competition prompt about slowing down. Coffee gives you a built-in pause — the minutes while it brews — and we usually spend them scrolling past our own lives.

sections.headline: A porch in Vietnam
sections.body: The real source is a childhood memory: my grandpa waiting for his coffee to brew on the porch. Sài Gòn coffee talk. The unhurried pace of Đà Nẵng. Vietnamese coffee culture treats waiting as part of the drink — Mugmood just carries that into a product.

sections.headline: Brew time becomes reflection time
sections.body:
While your coffee brews, Mugmood offers one gentle daily prompt — *"Write about something you're grateful for today."* Moka-pot iconography, soft stars, amber warmth. Nothing to optimize, no streaks to lose. Just a ritual.

*TODO(cherry): add phone mockups and the moka pot illustration.*

caseStudies.title: Tessera
caseStudies.oneliner: Turning fragmented in-between moments into simple, meaningful actions.
caseStudies.team: With Steven Yanzhi Chen, Anna Vorontsova, and Jiwan Park — Cornell UX Design Club (CUxD) hackathon
caseStudies.pullQuote: Use QuickStart to make the most of your small moments.

sections.headline: The day is full of tiles we throw away
sections.body: Waiting for the bus. Standing in line. The five minutes before a meeting. These fragments default to mindless scrolling — not because we choose it, but because nothing smaller than a feed fits the gap. A *tessera* is a single mosaic tile; the name is the thesis.

sections.headline: Small moments, simple actions
sections.body: Tessera offers actions sized to the gap: a quick reflection, a small goal, a distraction-free to-do with a timer. *QuickStart* matches the moment to the action so the choice never costs more than the moment itself.

I designed UI components and shaped the visual identity — the blue-to-lilac calm that keeps Tessera feeling like a breath, not another demand.

*TODO(cherry): add phone mockups.*

caseStudies.title: Wally
caseStudies.oneliner: A student wallet app — one place to learn, save, and manage money.
caseStudies.team: Team led by Aarya Mahapatra, with Ashley Cho, Jiwan Park, and Anna Vorontsova

sections.headline: Money is the class no one teaches
sections.body: College students are handed real financial stakes with no curriculum. Wally tackles financial literacy by putting learning, saving, and managing money in one friendly place — my first university UX project.

sections.headline: Drawing the guide
sections.body:
I designed **Wally himself** — the mascot who walks you through your money without judgment. Character design is where my illustration side meets product: a face makes a scary subject approachable in a way no empty state ever will.

*TODO(cherry): add Wally character sheets and app screens.*
